what_it_does: Implements the cross-session fabric layer for persistent channels, transient gatherings, durable connection-edge projections, and direct/conducted coordination modes. It persists channel lifecycle as an append-only JSONL registry under <store>/agent_sessions/channels.jsonl, folds that log into rows at read time, and routes channel posts by fanning per-member mail intents onto the existing mail leaf rather than creating a new message path.
observations: Module-level closed vocabularies CHANNEL_OPS, KINDS, MODES, PARTICIPATION, and ROW_STATUS are declared once and treated as registry-is-truth.
append_channel_event validates kind and channel id, assigns monotonic seq and ts under store.graph_lock, fsyncs, and returns the persisted record.
fold_channels rebuilds the current channel/gathering registry by replaying every event through _apply_channel_event.

create_channel mints ids as ch-N or ga-N under a mint lock, validates members through an optional registry callback, and supports origin provenance including promoted_from and parent sub-channel origins.

post_to_channel fans one mail intent per target member on store.append_agent_mail; supervised-live members get verb deliver, all others get verb queue, and the function never wakes or spawns sessions.
conducted mode sends a single intent to the coordinator session containing the channel context, roster live states, the message, and instructions for working members.
member_statuses composes awake/listening/busy/closed from declared posture, optional registry state, and an optional HTTP probe to the supervisor read face, reporting supervisor up/down/off in-band.
channel_history joins channel.posted events with mail leaf traffic on the same thread to surface posts and replies.
edges_for is a read-time projection over the durable mail leaf that yields direction-aware, verb-counted, channel-attributed connection edges with follow-up session_post handles.
The file deliberately writes no events.jsonl records, imports no supervisor, and spawns no sessions; all writes are store appends.
standouts: The routing law: channel posts do not invent a second message path; they fan to per-member records on the one mail leaf using existing store.append_agent_mail.
conducted mode delegates the whole channel post to a coordinator session as one structured intent, enabling recursive sub-channel command.
Connection edges are derived from mail.jsonl at query time, avoiding a separate durable write path.
Status composition is explicit about its sources and reports supervisor probe failure rather than silently degrading to a fresh-looking answer.
conventions: Follows an append-only log-is-the-index pattern with writer-assigned seq/ts and fsync under a graph lock.
Follows registry-is-truth by keeping closed vocabularies as module constants and rejecting unknown values with descriptive ValueErrors.
Injects an optional registry callback instead of importing a concrete session registry, keeping the module usable in tests and isolated contexts.
Breaks the typical registry-location convention by folding state in this file rather than suite.py; the docstring calls this an intentional, documented seam.
Prefers fail-loud validation over silent no-ops for unknown channels, duplicate members, invalid vocabularies, and empty required fields.
concerns: fold_channels re-reads the entire channels.jsonl leaf on every call; acceptable baseline but may need incremental caching if hot.
create_channel id minting scans existing rows under lock and never reuses archived/promoted ids, so id numbers grow and collision loop is O(n).
post_to_channel imports uuid lazily inside the function rather than at module top.
member_statuses catches all Exception on the supervisor probe with a bare pass, losing diagnostic detail beyond the reported supervisor: down.
edges_for scans the entire mail.jsonl for each query, making it O(mail volume) per call.
_apply_channel_event silently drops events for unknown channels instead of surfacing fold_errors or corrupt-leaf diagnostics.
notes: This file is the registry-shaped cross-session fabric: it turns an append-only channel log and the existing mail log into channels, gatherings, live member statuses, and durable edges without spawning sessions or writing events.jsonl.
questions: What is the exact contract of store methods such as append_agent_mail, agent_mail_since, put_content, get_content, and graph_lock?
How does the supervisor's /sessions endpoint represent state, and what does busy mean in its state machine?
Which production callers supply the registry callback, and how is it wired?
What is the intended behavior when a channel post's sender is not a member of the channel?
How are thread identifiers expected to avoid collisions between channel posts and direct session posts?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the cross-session fabric organs, append-only registry, routing law, conducted mode, and honest status composition exactly as described in the module docstring.
apparent_themes: Fan-out over existing primitives rather than new message paths.
Honest status composition with source attribution.
No second write path and no silent session side effects.
intention_signals: Build a lightweight, durable social/work topology around sessions by treating channels and gatherings as registry content, routing messages through the existing mail fabric, and deriving liveness and edges from authoritative sources rather than maintaining parallel mutable state.
novelty: The conducted coordination mode—routing an entire channel post to a single coordinator session as a structured intent that can recursively spawn sub-channels—and the explicit design rule that broadcast must not wake or spawn sessions, making the routing law a first-class constraint.
contribution: A minimal append-only channel/gathering registry with closed vocabularies, a fan-out router that reuses the existing mail leaf, a conducted-mode coordinator delegation primitive, and read-time projections for member status, channel history, and durable connection edges.
summary_for_embedding: company/runtime/session_channels.py implements an append-only registry and router for persistent channels and transient gatherings, fanning posts into per-member mail intents, supporting direct and conducted coordination modes, and projecting member status, channel history, and durable connection edges from the existing mail log.
intention_for_embedding: Provide a cross-session fabric layer that lets sessions form work-centric groups, message them without spawning new message paths, and follow up on who talked to whom while keeping the registry and routing honest about sources and side effects.
suggested_fields: channel_post_policy to capture the explicit no-wake/no-spawn broadcast rule.
supervisor_probe_schema to document expected /sessions response fields.
store_method_contracts to record assumed signatures of store methods.
thread_ownership_policy to clarify how threads are minted, joined, and reused.
proposed_edge_kinds: coordinates between a conducted channel and its coordinator session.
promoted_from between a promoted channel and its originating gathering.
validates_through between create_channel/add_member and the injected registry callback.

symbols.does: Returns an ISO 8601 UTC timestamp string.
symbols.description: Utility that produces a timezone-aware UTC datetime formatted as an ISO 8601 string, used as the canonical timestamp for channel lifecycle events.

symbols.does: Normalizes a session reference to session:// form.
symbols.description: Takes a session id string and ensures it carries the session:// prefix, providing a stable address form for session references.

symbols.does: Strips the session:// prefix from a session reference.
symbols.description: Returns the raw session id by removing the session:// scheme if present; used for comparisons and registry lookups.

symbols.does: Normalizes a channel reference to channel:// form.
symbols.description: Ensures a channel id carries the channel:// prefix, mirroring _addr for channels.

symbols.does: Strips the channel:// prefix from a channel reference.
symbols.description: Returns the raw channel id by removing the channel:// scheme if present; used for leaf keys and event matching.

symbols.does: Computes the channels.jsonl filesystem path.
symbols.description: Returns the Path to <store>/agent_sessions/channels.jsonl, centralizing the leaf location.

symbols.does: Appends one validated channel lifecycle event to channels.jsonl.
symbols.description: Validates kind against CHANNEL_OPS and channel id presence, assigns seq and ts under graph_lock, fsyncs the append, and returns the persisted record.

symbols.does: Reads channel events with seq greater than a cursor.
symbols.description: Returns oldest-first channel lifecycle events from channels.jsonl, optionally filtered by channel and limited; honest empty list if file missing.

symbols.does: Rebuilds channel/gathering rows from the append-only leaf.
symbols.description: Reads all channel events and applies them via _apply_channel_event to produce the current registry projection of channels and gatherings.

symbols.does: Updates one row based on a single channel event.
symbols.description: The single source of fold logic: handles created, member_added/removed/status, posted, mode_set, archived, dispersed, and promoted events.

symbols.does: Returns one folded channel row or raises ValueError.
symbols.description: Looks up a channel by bare id in the folded registry and fails loud if unknown, refusing to fabricate a channel.

symbols.does: Raises if a channel row is not active.
symbols.description: Guard used by mutating operations to refuse writes against archived, dispersed, or promoted channels with a descriptive error.

symbols.does: Creates a channel or gathering and returns its folded row.
symbols.description: Validates name, kind, mode, participation vocabulary, and optional registry membership; mints a kind-prefixed id under lock; appends channel.created; supports origin and conducted coordinator requirements.

symbols.does: Adds a session to an active channel.
symbols.description: Validates participation vocabulary, refuses re-adding existing members, optionally validates session via registry, and appends channel.member_added.

symbols.does: Removes a session from an active channel.
symbols.description: Refuses removal of non-members and refuses removal of a conducted channel's coordinator without first changing mode, then appends channel.member_removed.

symbols.does: Sets a member's declared participation posture.
symbols.description: Updates a member's stored awake/listening posture on an active channel, failing loud if the posture vocabulary or membership is invalid.

symbols.does: Changes a channel's coordination mode.
symbols.description: Switches between direct and conducted modes, validating that a conducted mode has a member coordinator, and appends channel.mode_set.

symbols.does: Terminates a channel with archived status.
symbols.description: Appends channel.archived for active durable channels only; gatherings must disperse or promote.

symbols.does: Terminates a gathering with dispersed status.
symbols.description: Appends gathering.dispersed for active gatherings only; leaves history readable but the gathering is gone.

symbols.does: Promotes a gathering to a durable channel.
symbols.description: Creates a new channel carrying over members and posture, stamps promoted_from origin, then marks the gathering promoted with promoted_to.

symbols.does: Routes a message to channel members via the mail leaf.
symbols.description: Validates message and sender, chooses targets by mode, mints a shared thread, fans one mail intent per target on store.append_agent_mail with deliver/queue verbs, and appends channel.posted with fan details.

symbols.does: Looks up a session's registry state for routing.
symbols.description: Nested helper inside post_to_channel that calls the optional registry callback and returns the session state, mapping lookup failures to None so routing falls back to queue.

symbols.does: Projects per-member status from declared, registry, and supervisor sources.
symbols.description: Composes awake/listening/busy/closed status per member, optionally probing the supervisor HTTP read face, and reports the supervisor probe state and status_source honestly.

symbols.does: Returns a channel's posts joined with related mail traffic.
symbols.description: Collects channel.posted events since a cursor, resolves message bodies, and joins the mail leaf by thread to surface replies and coordinator traffic.

symbols.does: Projects durable connection edges for a session from the mail leaf.
symbols.description: Scans mail.jsonl for records involving the session, aggregates direction, verbs, threads, and channel attribution, and provides follow-up session_post args for addressable peers.
